headline: 3 Answers That Burned Carnival Stock Investors This Week
summary: The cruise line giant slips after failing to make waves this earnings season.

headline: Carnival Corporation Expands Surplus Meal Donation Program to the Dominican Republic
summary: Carnival Corporation (NYSE: CCL), the world's largest cruise company, today announced the expansion of its surplus meal donation program to the Dominican Republic, marking the second Latin American country to join the company's initiative to safely redirect prepared, unserved meals to local communities where its ships visit.

headline: Price Prediction: Carnival Has 31% Upside as Q2 Earnings Beat Masks Opportunity
summary: Carnival Corporation (NYSE:CCL) just delivered its twelfth consecutive quarter of record net yields, yet the stock sold off after Q2 results landed. That dislocation is the setup for our call. Our 24/7 Wall St. price target for Carnival is $37.74 over the next 12 months, implying 31.41% upside from a current price of $28.72. The ... Price Prediction: Carnival Has 31% Upside as Q2 Earnings Beat Masks Opportunity

headline: CARNIVAL CRUISE LINE UNVEILS THE NEXT COURSE: A NEW WAVE OF EXCITING CULINARY EXPERIENCES AT SEA
summary: Carnival Cruise Line revealed The Next Course, a new wave of culinary experiences featuring restaurants and bars launching on Carnival Festivale in 2027 and Carnival Tropicale in 2028, and with new innovative dining experiences now being rolled out across the fleet.

headline: Carnival (CCL) Bans 16 Guests After Customs Checkpoint Altercation
summary: Carnival barred 16 guests from future cruises after a serious altercation at a customs checkpoint. The incident occurred as guests were disembarking, leading the company to enforce its conduct and safety policies. The decision highlights Carnival's zero tolerance approach to behavior that affects passenger and crew safety. Carnival, traded as NYSE:CCL, is drawing attention for this operational decision at a time when its share price stands at $28.91. The stock is up 11.3% over the past 30...

headline: Wells Fargo Maintains Overweight on Carnival, Raises Price Target to $38
summary: Wells Fargo  analyst Trey Bowers   maintains Carnival (NYSE:CCL) with a Overweight and raises the price target from $36 to $38.

headline: 3 Large-Cap Stocks with Warning Signs
summary: Large-cap stocks have the power to shape entire industries thanks to their size and widespread influence. With such vast footprints, however, finding new areas for growth is much harder than for smaller, more agile players.

headline: CCL Stock Gains Premarket: Wall Street Stays Bullish On Carnival’s Cruise Demand Despite Europe Headwinds
summary: Analysts offered mixed price target changes after Carnival’s second-quarter results, but remained positive on long-term demand.
